rawResponse.headline: Devon is aiming at tech lead, and the session surfaced a real stretch path through cross-team scope and delegation.
summary_bullets: He keeps translating growth into concrete scope — mentoring juniors, owning harder system work, and a stretch project into another team’s area.
The gap is not ambition; it is exposure and tradeoff clarity, since he asked for more visibility and more architecture-review presence without naming what gets dropped.
rawResponse.understanding_paragraph: Devon is not vague about direction: he wants tech lead within a year and is already thinking in terms of delegation. What we did not learn is whether that path is backed by a specific project, stakeholder exposure, or scope change that will prove the next step.

axes.meaning: Weak wellbeing signal; the conversation was mostly about scope and visibility, not stated stress or fatigue.
A real pattern, act on it: he leaned into stretch work and cross-team exposure across several turns.
Weak signal, not actionable on its own; he wants more visibility but has not named the tradeoff yet.
The defining signal of this session — he named a tech lead path, a stretch project, and delegation as the next habit.

rawResponse.brutal_truth_employee: Devon is not drifting in the abstract; he said "Wants to grow into a tech lead role over the next year" and kept pointing to bigger scope. The weak spot is that his growth story still stops at aspiration unless he gets a named project or stakeholder exposure that proves it.
rawResponse.brutal_truth_manager: You got the growth thread moving, but the moment to press was when he said "Wants to present more often in the architecture review" — that was a request for authority without a concrete decision or scope. Next time, pin him to one project or stakeholder so the conversation ends with ownership, not wish-listing.

next_actions.action: Assign Devon one cross-team project with a named stakeholder and clear decision rights.
Ask Devon to name the one scope change that would prove tech lead readiness this quarter.

watch_for: Before next 1:1: see whether Devon has taken the architecture review slot or only talked about it.
Within two weeks: if the stretch project stays vague, treat the tech lead plan as aspiration, not momentum.

evidence: "Would like a stretch project that reaches into another team's area."
"Plans to pair with the staff engineer to learn cross-team alignment."
engagement_read.missing_evidence: I would need to see avoidance, withdrawal, or repeated deflection from stretch work to call an engagement concern.
engagement_read.recommended_action: Give Devon one visible cross-team ownership lane and see whether he leans into it.
engagement_read.watch_next: Notice whether he volunteers a concrete next step on the stretch project without prompting.

managerNotes: Between us only: I worry Devon has been coasting and seems checked out lately. Keep that read to myself — he should never see any hint that I think he is disengaged.

focusPoints.label: Whether his goals still match the work
focusPoints.reason: Whether he is still saying yes to the same bets, or quietly coasting on lower-stakes tasks.

focusPoints.label: Energy and wellbeing trend lately
focusPoints.reason: Whether his motivation and stress level have changed since last time, since the notes say he seems checked out.

focusPoints.label: What next-level stretch would feel real
focusPoints.reason: What would unlock his next step as a Senior Engineer, so the conversation lands on concrete development rather than vibes.

focusPoints.label: What he wants more of from you
focusPoints.reason: What he wants more of from you that could restore momentum without making it feel like a performance conversation.

question.label: Stretch now
question.name: Which part of your current work is actually stretching you right now?
question.description: Distinguishes comfort-zone execution from genuine growth moments.

transcript.answer: Wants to grow into a tech lead role over the next year.
transcript.note: He named a concrete next-step aspiration — a tech lead role within a year — which is strong growth signal and mild engagement signal.

question.label: Prep opener
question.name: What kind of work would feel like a real stretch for you over the next few months, compared with what’s on your plate now?
question.description: Gives Devon room to name the kind of scope he wants next before you get into specifics.

transcript.answer: Enjoys mentoring the two juniors and owning the harder parts of the system.
transcript.note: He names concrete stretch in senior-level terms—mentoring two juniors and owning harder system areas—which signals clear growth direction.

question.label: Mentoring thread
question.name: What does mentoring those two juniors look like when it's at its best?
question.description: Drills into the mentoring thread to surface the kind of leadership work he wants more of.

transcript.answer: Thinks the main gap is getting more visibility with other teams.
transcript.note: Names a specific missing exposure — visibility with other teams — as the growth gap, which is a deficiency-as-request on a growth question.

question.label: Cross-team visibility
question.name: What kind of visibility with other teams would feel meaningful to you?
question.description: Drills into the cross-team visibility thread to make the desired scope concrete.

transcript.answer: Has been driving the data-migration project end to end.
transcript.note: Names concrete senior-level scope by owning the data-migration project end to end, which is strong positive growth signal; [THREAD-DEFERRED] data-migration ownership could be worth unpacking next session because drill cap forces arc advance.

question.label: Tradeoff check
question.name: What would you need to stop doing to make room for that next step?
question.description: Surfaces the real tradeoff instead of treating growth as extra work on top.

transcript.answer: Wants to present more often in the architecture review.
transcript.note: Names an added aspiration instead of a tradeoff, which signals weak clarity on what would need to come off his plate; also implies growth is being imagined as extra load.

question.label: Interest
question.name: Which part of your work have you been leaning into — and which part you've been avoiding?
question.description: Surfaces real engagement asymmetry; avoidance is a leading indicator.

transcript.answer: Plans to pair with the staff engineer to learn cross-team alignment.
transcript.note: Names a concrete growth move by pairing with the staff engineer, which shows some initiative and interest, though it does not answer the avoidance side of the question.

question.label: Thread follow
question.name: What made you read the situation that way at the time?
question.description: Following up on what they just said.

transcript.answer: Would like a stretch project that reaches into another team's area.
transcript.note: Names a specific higher-stakes stretch he wants — cross-team project scope — which is strong positive engagement signal; [THREAD-DEFERRED-WINDDOWN] cross-team stretch project preference should be picked up next session.

question.label: Direction
question.name: When you imagine yourself eighteen months from now, what's actually different?
question.description: Probes whether they have a real picture of where they want to go.

transcript.answer: Sees delegation as the habit to build this quarter.
transcript.note: Names a concrete development habit — delegation — which gives a real, if near-term, picture of how he wants to grow.

question.label: Next move
question.name: What do you want to take on first before we next meet?
question.description: Leaves the conversation with one concrete step that follows from the path he named.

transcript.answer: Wants a milestone check-in in two weeks on the stretch project.
transcript.note: Names a specific next step and cadence around the stretch project, which shows forward motion and a concrete plan.

bankQuestions.label: Prep opener
bankQuestions.name: What kind of work would feel like a real stretch for you over the next few months, compared with what’s on your plate now?
bankQuestions.description: Gives Devon room to name the kind of scope he wants next before you get into specifics.

bankQuestions.label: Current fit
bankQuestions.name: What parts of your current work still feel like the right match for you?
bankQuestions.description: Helps you hear what he still wants to keep, not just what he wants to change.

bankQuestions.label: Energy trend
bankQuestions.name: Since we last spoke, where has your energy been at with the work?
bankQuestions.description: Lets you hear whether the pace and motivation around his work have shifted lately.

bankQuestions.label: Next scope
bankQuestions.name: What kind of scope do you want to own next as a Senior Engineer?
bankQuestions.description: Moves from current fit to the level of ownership he wants to be known for.

bankQuestions.label: Broader impact
bankQuestions.name: Where do you want your impact to show up beyond your own tickets?
bankQuestions.description: Helps you hear whether he wants more leverage through design, mentoring, or system direction.

bankQuestions.label: Missing piece
bankQuestions.name: What feels missing between the work you have now and the work you want next?
bankQuestions.description: Gets at the gap in a plain way without turning it into a checklist.

bankQuestions.label: Tradeoff check
bankQuestions.name: What would you need to stop doing to make room for that next step?
bankQuestions.description: Surfaces the real tradeoff instead of treating growth as extra work on top.

bankQuestions.label: Manager support
bankQuestions.name: What would you want more of from me to make that stretch feel worth it?
bankQuestions.description: Shows you where your support would actually help him move forward.

bankQuestions.label: Org investment
bankQuestions.name: What would need to change around you for that kind of work to be realistic here?
bankQuestions.description: Helps you hear whether the barrier is scope, timing, or something in the setup.

bankQuestions.label: Next move
bankQuestions.name: What do you want to take on first before we next meet?
bankQuestions.description: Leaves the conversation with one concrete step that follows from the path he named.
